source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The picture on the right shows a tile pattern. The side length of the bigger tiles is a and of the smaller ones b. The dotted lines (horizontal and tilted) include an angle of $30^{\circ}$. How big is the ratio a:b?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The picture on the right shows a tile pattern'
the task depends on a geometric tile arrangement, dotted lines, and a 30-degree visual angle relation
multi_step: solving requires extracting geometric relationships from the tile pattern before deriving the ratio a:b
the 30-degree angle must be combined with side-length variables a and b, indicating at least two linked reasoning steps
multiple-choice options are all close-form ratios, so the intended solution is not simple lookup but geometric derivation
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests upstream expectation of nontrivial reasoning potential
verifiability: answer_present=true and given as an exact ratio '(2+sqrt(3)):1'-style closed form
choices include the same exact ratio, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
answer_type=short_text with a precise symbolic target is easy to compare or normalize
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and no registry issues are listed

risk_flags: diagram-dependent geometry may be sensitive to image interpretation

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, and the asset registry passed with no listed issues
initial scoring is strong: high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning potential, and high verifiability
the exact short-text answer and matching multiple-choice structure make final-answer checking straightforward
although the problem is diagram-dependent and may be somewhat sensitive to image interpretation, the core semantic assets are intact and sufficient for collection
